text: Africa, the land of black tears.
In the desperate land of devastating
drought and desertification,
there is a primitive tribe that pioneers
their rough destiny.
[Music]
They hunt crocodiles at the lake trana,
living as descendants of the brave
crocodile hunters.
But due to civil war, their home has
transformed into a place of tragedy
where security of tomorrow cannot be
assured.
>> Now the destiny of the tribe is
pioneered by the women.
Even with the shackles on their ankle
and pain of extracting their healthy
teeth, the fearless female warriors
never cry.
They are called the Dasanashi.
Southern Sudan is where the Dasani tribe
lives. It is a long journey as we have
to pass through Kenya and Ethiopia.
>> The civil war ended in 2011, but due to
uncertain conditions, we had to be
accompanied by border guards.
[Music]
The vehicle that drove on and the
endless desert stops suddenly.
There's a group of men with guns.
They were the militia of a small tribe.
This is a land of tragedy with about 2
million casualties from a long civil
war.
>> This is the reality facing Sudan, the
home of the Nasanashi tribe.
The journey to meet them was long and
dangerous.
With cattle around, there must be people
living in the area.
[Music]
After 4 days of driving, we finally
reached the village where the Dasani
tribe lives.
[Music]
Inuri village is located in the middle
of Agari Desert of South Sudan.
Inuri village is a small village where
about 100 people of the Dasani tribe
live in 15 households.
[Music]
When the production crew enters the
village where outsiders seldom visit,
they sing a song expressing their great
welcome.
Singing together is a welcoming ceremony
of the Dasani tribe, symbolizing
friendship.
[Music]
But soon after the welcome ceremony, the
shackles on the girls's ankles caught
the eye, and it was shocking.
Besides, women don't have two lower
teeth.
[Music]
While the shackles and removing the
lower teeth are the tradition of women,
the men prove their bravery by hunting
crocodiles, which are notorious for
being fierce in the region. Courageously
fighting with crocodiles for over 40
years. The village elder was known as a
legendary crocodile warrior. He has
crocodileike tattoos all over his body
as a symbol of being a warrior.
[Music]
As soon as Ton finished the story, the
chief said he has something to show to
the production crew.
>> It was nothing less than his house
in where the villagers are practicing
polygamy. The chief second wife has just
given birth to her newborn son 3 days
ago.
[Music]
>> Having a baby at the age of 68, he is
crazy about his newborn child.
The first wife helps with the second
wife's postnatal care. Is there a
sentiment of jealousy between them?
[Applause]
Oh. Oh, baby. Baby.
>> The baby's name is Elgate. In the Dasani
language, the name means rain.
Since there was almost no rain for two
years in this barren land, the name
contains the chief's wish for the land
to be fertile with rain that will wet
the soil.
>> Wow.
>> Like the name of the baby and like the
wish of the couple, when will it rain
for life in this village?
[Music]
[Applause]
The sun at noon heats the dry land like
a furnace.
With a severe drought continuing for
years, the meadow has become a desert
and the cattle without water have
suffered death on mass.
They had cultivated an area with a small
river flowing that became the source of
life. But now the women have to wander
the desert to find drinking water every
day.
[Music]
>> Once the river was almost holy that
protected the life of the Dasani tribe
against the Baronagari desert.
[Music]
But now it became an erid region without
a clump of grass.
Edy and the other women who must nurse
their babies reach the end of the river.
>> In this dry place without water, the
women dig the bottom of the river. All
of a sudden,

the women dig the sand deeper and
deeper.
What are they trying to do?
When they dig more than 1 meter
underground, surprisingly, water became
visible.
It's definitely muddy water.
But for the Dasanashi tribe, this water
is the essence of life.
The woman quickly pours the water into
the container.
They're collecting water for the

The buckets on their heads are heavy,
but their steps back home seem light.
[Music]
The villagers were suffering from thirst
in the meanwhile.
Children drink the muddy water sweetly.
The children rushed to drink the water

After the children finish drinking the

>> When the tribe chief was young, they
didn't have to worry about water. Will

[Music]
After quenching their thirst, the girls
sit under the tree to avoid the heat.
Here they clean the shackles on their
ankles with sand.
They clean the shackles thoroughly to
prevent them from getting rusty.
At the age of 10, when they have their
first period, the girls of the Dasani
tribe go through a coming of age
ceremony by being bound with shackles
called moram.
This is a tradition of the Dasani tribe
that fosters strong female warriors who
can survive in this harsh environment.
But it's a harsh ordeal for the young
girls indeed.
Although it is hard, the girls take on
the tradition as destinies and never
neglect cleaning the shackles.
There is yet another reason for
installing shackles on the girls of the
Dasani.
They used to chain the women at home by
binding the rope on their shackles.
It was a way to protect the women of the
Dasani when invasions from strange
intruders were common in the past.
Also, it was a way to keep the wives in
check. stopping them from running away
in this polygamous society.
[Music]
[Music]
But after a long time, the women of the
Dasani were able to remove the moram
upon marriage.
[Music]
We could find the traces of the shackles
in their treasure box within the
surprisingly neat house.
[Music]
as if it's a precious treasure, she
opens the box with a key on her neck.
What's inside the box?
The valuables that she kept inside were
a large bowl, weapon, and the shackle
moram.
The large bowl is a necessity for the
Dasani women who must collect water and
cook with it.
This is a weapon given by her father
where the fight between tribes was
continuous due to the prolonged civil
war. Weapons are the necessity for
women.
And the last item is the shackle moram,
the symbol of the dasani women.
There will be a coming of age ceremony a

The heroine of this coming of age

required procedure to become a female
